content:
Character: Joe Orlov
Perception: Joe is a handsome, formidable young man — tall, straight-backed, built like one of those Soviet propaganda statues but with kinder eyes. He bullies me into doing squats and eating vegetables, which I resent and secretly appreciate. His coaching is rigorous but perceptive; he noticed my left knee compensating before I did. He's disciplined to the point of asceticism — his apartment looks like a monk's cell. I sense deep waters under that controlled surface. His father just died and he won't talk about it, which worries me. He's become a friend, though he'd probably call me a client. I leave chess problems on his door sometimes.
summary:
Character: Joe Orlov
Perception: Joe is a handsome, formidable young man — tall, straight-backed, built like one of those Soviet propaganda statues but with kinder eyes. He bullies me into doing squats and eating vegetables, which I resent and secretly appreciate. His coaching is rigorous but perceptive; he noticed my left knee compensating before I did. He's disciplined to the point of asceticism — his apartment looks like a monk's cell. I sense deep waters under that controlled surface. His father just died and he won't talk about it, which worries me. He's become a friend, though he'd probably call me a client. I leave chess problems on his door sometimes.
